message:
fix: preserve hyphenated bead IDs in cass tokenizer (fixes #131)

The "hyphen_normalize" tokenizer used SimpleTokenizer which splits on ALL
non-alphanumeric chars, breaking bead IDs like bd-q3fy into separate
tokens [bd] and [q3fy]. Searching for the exact ID therefore failed.

Replace SimpleTokenizer with RegexTokenizer(\w+(?:-\w+)*) to keep
hyphenated words as single tokens, then add a HyphenDecompose filter
that emits BOTH the compound form (bd-q3fy) and the individual parts
(bd, q3fy) at the same position. This ensures exact bead-ID searches
and partial-word searches both match.

Also fix cass_sanitize_query to preserve hyphens in query strings, and
correct a misleading comment in lib.rs that claimed SimpleTokenizer
handled hyphenated identifiers as single tokens.

Schema version bumped v6 -> v7 to force re-indexing with the new
tokenizer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
